parsed_review.summary:
CP00-765 is a descriptor-only RP25 Migration Platform interface/permission/fixture pack (risk B, 40 units, RP25.P03.M04.S13-RP25.P03.M06.S10). The pack-level claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with descriptor-level flag migration_platform_interface_permission_fixture_descriptor_verified; it does NOT overclaim runtime or enterprise trust, and that claim is fully supported by implementation, tests, local/Hermes validation, and the no-write/no-real-data boundary.

Verified consistency across all layers: registry.js CP765 binding (lines 162-177), service.js descriptor factory (715-770), validators.js coverage+descriptor checks (821-889), index.js export surface, model.test.js CP765 tests (362-408), README CP00-765 section (line 41-43), contract current_pack/latest_pack (lines 24-55), and plan live_state (line 35-41) all agree on pack_id, risk_class B, unit_count 40, range, upstream CP00-764, next CP00-766 / RP25.P03.M06.S11. Deliverable distribution (test:4, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2, implementation:19, contract:9, security_audit:4) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice:8, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:10) match the pack baseline exactly; contract validation block (lines 32067-32129) shows coverage.valid=true and descriptor.valid=true with empty errors and validation.valid=true.

Tests and gates: command-evidence.json reports every required command at exit_code 0 (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check). node --test migration suite = 31/31 pass; full npm test = 2979/2979 pass, 0 fail; git diff --check clean. command-evidence status=passed, gate_outcome=passed_pending_hardened_review, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false.

Boundaries: MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js 193-207) asserts descriptor_only/synthetic_only/no real data and all source-system + product-state write surfaces closed; validators enforce descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no raw/source/credential/client payloads, no source-system runtime opening, no permission-decision/cross-tenant exposure, no audit event bodies; tests assert the same. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false. Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor/receipt handling (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_migration_no_write); no Hermes runtime embedded in product. The contract validation script writes only the contract artifact, not product state.

Review-evidence hardening (CP178+): the existing claude-review-result.json is a 0-byte-stdout placeholder with status pending_review and null execution fields, but it is explicitly NOT counted as valid evidence (manifest pack_level_claude_review.valid_review_receipts=[], adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json both PENDING_REVIEW with production_ready=false). No auth failure, malformed JSON, fenced-JSON-with-prose, tool-call-shaped output, or missing-field artifact is being passed off as a valid review receipt; the pending state is tracked correctly and this hardened read-only receipt is the gating input. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not final approver. Changed-file scope is accurate (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]) and unrelated dirty files (workbook docs, progress-control-room, plan/queue regen) are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found; clean PASS contingent on this receipt being recorded by the closeout flow.
